game_name: Might and Magic VII: For Blood and Honor

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players choose 4 characters from races (Dwarf, Elf, Goblin, Human) and classes (including new Thief, Monk, Ranger)
Initiating quest that grounds party in world and gives them base of operations
Multiple distinct regions: AvLee (elves), Tularean Forest, Deyja, Tatalia, Bracada, Celeste, Mount Nighon, Emerald Isle, Evernmorn, Barrow Downs (dwarves), Erathia (humans), Harmondale
Tensions escalating in Harmondale; sets stage for mandatory alignment choice
Class-restricted skills capped by expertise: Novice → Expert → Master → Grandmaster. Different classes reach different caps
Expertise level in school determines available spells; examples include 'Fly' spell requiring Air Magic Mastery, with Grandmaster benefit of zero mana cost
Optional but with consequences; affects party's realm if completed within set time limit
Finding successor to Harmondale's Arbiter; defines rest of game. Affects display color, quest availability, NPC faction relations, promotion quest paths
Tier 1 promotions available anytime; Tier 2 promotions require alignment choice. Example: Paladin → Crusader → (Hero if Light / Villain if Dark)
Choosing Light makes all Dark-aligned town guards hostile and vice versa; chosen alignment city becomes friendly
Tower-building minigame tied to taverns across Antagarich; optional side-quest with card collection requirement
Eight heroes from Might and Magic III attempting to pursue Guardians Corak and Sheltem; crash-landed on Enroth; plot threads continue from previous games

typical_tension_triggers: Regional transitions with escalating political conflict
Exploration of dangerous regions (The Pit necromancer stronghold, Mount Nighon)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Political tension gradually escalates from early regional exploration through mandatory mid-game alignment choice, then branches into faction-locked content with consequences for NPC relations and promotion paths through late game

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Taverns across Antagarich accessible for Arcomage minigame; optional time-limited quests with consequences

difficulty_curve.early_game: Character customization and scavenger hunt establish baseline; regional exploration with manageable political context
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mandatory alignment choice creates branching difficulty; hostile faction mechanics introduce penalty zones
difficulty_curve.late_game: Faction-locked promotions force commitment; ancient Terra hero storyline convergence increases narrative stakes
notable_spikes: Mandatory Light/Dark alignment choice (commits player to restricted quest content and hostile zones)
Transition to second-tier class promotions (requires completed alignment path)
Necromancer stronghold (The Pit) exploration in Deyja barrens

notable_level_patterns.description: Game world divided into distinct regional zones (AvLee, Tularean Forest, Deyja, Tatalia, Bracada, Celeste, Mount Nighon, Emerald Isle, Evernmorn, Barrow Downs, Erathia, Harmondale), each with distinct faction presence and thematic geography (elven lands, dwarven lands, deserts, mountains, swamps, cloud cities, barrens)
Post-alignment choice, party encounters either Light-friendly or Dark-friendly cities; rival faction zones become hostile; quest availability and character promotion paths split based on path chosen
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple aligned cities (Light vs Dark pairs)

notable_level_patterns.description: Arcomage card game available in taverns across all regions; tied to optional side-quests requiring card collection and play in multiple tavern locations; provides mechanical relief and progression alternative
Tier 1 promotions earned anytime; Tier 2 promotions gated behind mandatory alignment choice; creates narrative pacing where class advancement unlocks are tied to story commitment
